questions.question: Which two elements can you add to a Notion page to make it more visually appealing?

options.text: Page icons and cover images
options.explanation: Page icons and cover images are explicitly mentioned as ways to enhance visual appeal.

options.text: Embedded spreadsheets and code blocks
options.explanation: While useful, these are not highlighted as visual enhancements in the guide.

options.text: Comments and mentions
options.explanation: These are collaboration tools, not visual design elements.

options.text: Database relations and rollups
options.explanation: These are functional features, not visual styling options.

questions.question: How can you transform a regular block into a quote, heading, or callout?

options.text: Right‑click the block and choose "Convert To"
options.explanation: The guide specifies using the six‑dot icon, not right‑click.

options.text: Click the block’s six‑dot icon, select "Turn Into", then pick the desired type
options.explanation: This matches the exact steps described in the article.

options.text: Press Ctrl + Shift + T while the block is selected
options.explanation: No keyboard shortcut is mentioned for this action.

options.text: Drag the block onto the sidebar and drop it as a new block type
options.explanation: Dragging to the sidebar creates a new block, not a transformation of the existing one.

questions.question: What purpose does adding background colors to section headings serve?

options.text: It makes the headings easier to locate and distinguishes sections visually
options.explanation: Background colors help headings stand out, improving readability.

options.text: It automatically groups related blocks into a database
options.explanation: Coloring does not affect block organization beyond visual cues.

options.text: It enables conditional formatting based on page content
options.explanation: Notion does not support conditional formatting for headings.

options.text: It locks the heading so it cannot be edited
options.explanation: Coloring does not change edit permissions.

questions.question: Which action allows you to create columns on a Notion page?

options.text: Select two blocks, click the six‑dot icon, and drag one block beside the other until a blue line appears
options.explanation: Dragging blocks next to each other with the blue guide line creates columns.

options.text: Insert a "Column" block from the slash command menu
options.explanation: Notion does not have a dedicated column block; columns are formed by positioning blocks side‑by‑side.

options.text: Apply a "Two‑Column Layout" template to the page
options.explanation: Templates can provide pre‑made columns, but the guide describes manual block placement.

options.text: Enable the "Grid View" in page settings
options.explanation: Grid view is a database view, not a method for column layout on regular pages.

questions.question: Why might a well‑designed Notion page be beneficial for a team?

options.text: It clarifies information, showcases work, and improves team engagement
options.explanation: The guide states that a nicely designed page helps bring work and the team behind it to life.

options.text: It automatically generates project timelines based on content
options.explanation: Design does not create timelines; that requires specific database features.

options.text: It increases the page's loading speed
options.explanation: Visual customization generally adds assets, which can slightly affect load time, not improve speed.

options.text: It grants each team member edit permissions by default
options.explanation: Permissions are managed separately from visual design.

questions.question: If you want to add a visual cue that loops, which type of media should you use?

options.text: GIF
options.explanation: GIFs are mentioned as visual cues that can animate, providing a looping effect.

options.text: Static image
options.explanation: Static images do not loop.

options.text: PDF document
options.explanation: PDFs are not visual cues and cannot loop.

options.text: Audio file
options.explanation: Audio does not provide a visual looping cue.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a step described for moving a block on a page?

options.text: Click the block’s six‑dot icon to pick it up
options.explanation: This is part of the described process.

options.text: Drag the block while watching for blue guide lines
options.explanation: Blue lines are mentioned as visual guides during dragging.

options.text: Hold Shift while dragging to duplicate the block
options.explanation: The guide does not mention using Shift to duplicate; duplication requires a separate command.

options.text: Drop the block in the desired location
options.explanation: Dropping completes the move as described.

questions.question: When browsing templates, where does the guide suggest you look?

options.text: The template gallery with over 10,000 options
options.explanation: The guide explicitly points to the template gallery.

options.text: The Notion community forum
options.explanation: The forum is not mentioned for template browsing.

options.text: The YouTube channel’s video descriptions
options.explanation: YouTube is listed for subscription, not for template browsing.

options.text: The help center’s FAQ section
options.explanation: The FAQ is for help, not template browsing.

questions.question: Which combination of actions would you use to turn a paragraph into a callout with a custom background color?

options.text: Select the paragraph, click the six‑dot icon, choose "Turn Into" → "Callout", then apply a background color via the heading settings
options.explanation: First you transform the block into a callout, then you can add a background color to the heading within the callout.

options.text: Right‑click the paragraph, choose "Add Background", then select "Callout" from the menu
options.explanation: Right‑click does not provide these options according to the guide.

options.text: Drag the paragraph onto a cover image; the system auto‑converts it to a callout with color
options.explanation: Dragging onto a cover image does not trigger conversion.

options.text: Insert a new callout block and copy‑paste the paragraph text, then change the callout’s icon to a color
options.explanation: While possible, the guide’s direct method is to use "Turn Into".

questions.question: What does the guide suggest you do if you encounter a feature that wasn’t covered in the article?

options.text: Visit the help center for more information
options.explanation: The "Something we didn’t cover?" section lists visiting the help center as a next step.

options.text: Submit a bug report via the Notion feedback button
options.explanation: Bug reporting isn’t mentioned for uncovered features.

options.text: Post a question on the Notion subreddit
options.explanation: The guide points to official resources, not external forums.

options.text: Contact the Notion sales team directly
options.explanation: Sales contact is unrelated to help for feature usage.
